inputs.userGoal: Deploy a real-time recommendation engine as an Azure ML managed online endpoint, scale for high request volumes, and store usage activity in Cosmos DB. Assume all defaults to make the plan.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required dependency for Azure ML workspace. Storage account provides blob storage for datasets, models, and logs. Standard_LRS provides cost-effective local redundancy suitable for ML workloads.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard_LRS balances cost and performance. No geo-redundancy but acceptable for ML artifacts that can be recreated.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required dependency for Azure ML workspace. Stores encryption keys, secrets, and certificates. Standard SKU meets requirements for ML workspace integration.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier sufficient for ML workspace requirements. Premium adds HSM support at higher cost.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required dependency for Azure ML workspace. Provides monitoring, logging, and diagnostics for ML endpoints. Web type suitable for HTTP endpoint monitoring.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Basic tier provides essential monitoring capabilities. Workspace-based offers advanced analytics at higher cost.

resources.name: mlw-recsys-prod-001

properties.description: ML workspace for recommendation engine model deployment and management

reasoning.whyChosen: Core service for deploying and managing ML models as managed online endpoints. Provides model registry, deployment automation, and endpoint management. Basic SKU sufficient for production workloads.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Basic tier provides essential ML capabilities. Standard offers advanced features like private link at higher cost.

resources.dependencyReasoning: ML workspace requires linked storage account for artifacts, Key Vault for secrets management, and Application Insights for monitoring.

resources.name: ml-endpoint-recsys-prod

properties.description: Real-time recommendation engine endpoint for high-volume inference

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides managed, scalable real-time inference for recommendation models. Standard_DS3_v2 offers good CPU/memory balance for recommendation workloads. Auto-scaling enables handling variable request volumes.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard_DS3_v2 balances performance and cost. Can scale horizontally for high volumes. GPU SKUs would increase cost significantly.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Online endpoints must be deployed within an ML workspace.

reasoning.whyChosen: Ideal for storing usage activity data with high throughput and low latency requirements. Session consistency balances performance and data consistency. Auto-failover ensures high availability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Cosmos DB provides better scalability and global distribution than SQL Database. Higher cost than Table Storage but offers superior query capabilities and performance.

resources.name: cosmos-recsys-db

reasoning.whyChosen: Database to organize usage activity collections. Autoscale throughput automatically adjusts to demand patterns, optimizing cost while handling traffic spikes.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Autoscale costs more per RU but provides automatic scaling. Better for variable workloads than fixed provisioning.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Database must be created within the Cosmos DB account.

resources.name: cosmos-recsys-usage-container

reasoning.whyChosen: Container to store user interaction and usage activity data. Partitioned by userId for optimal distribution. Consistent indexing ensures query performance for real-time analytics.
reasoning.tradeoffs: UserId partitioning provides good distribution for user-centric queries. May create hot partitions for highly active users but generally well-distributed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Container must be created within the database.

overallReasoning.summary: Architecture provides a scalable real-time recommendation system using Azure ML managed endpoints for inference and Cosmos DB for activity storage. ML workspace manages model lifecycle and endpoint deployment. Auto-scaling capabilities handle variable request volumes.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Balanced cost and performance by using Standard/Basic SKUs where appropriate. Autoscale features increase cost but provide automatic capacity management. Session consistency in Cosmos DB trades some consistency for better performance.

plan.validation: All resources are properly configured with required dependencies. ML workspace has all required linked services. Online endpoint configured for auto-scaling. Cosmos DB configured with appropriate partitioning and consistency for usage tracking workload.
